article_meta.title: Democracy Dies in DarknessPoliticsDonald TrumpWhite HouseDemocracy in AmericaCourts & LawNational SecurityElectionsPolit

reasoning: Mostly neutral and attributed to experts, but it adopts and amplifies the article’s framing in places (e.g., “increasingly militarized and aggressive tactics,” “resorted to deadly methods when less-lethal alternatives were viable”) without clear attribution, presenting subjective characterizations as fact.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses interpretive phrasing like “crucial tactical missteps” and “resorted to deadly methods,” rather than strictly neutral verbs.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States that less-lethal alternatives were viable and that tactics were increasingly militarized/aggressive without explicit attribution.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Presents descriptions of tactics as “increasingly militarized and aggressive” in the model’s voice rather than attributing to critics.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Employs emotionally charged terms (militarized, aggressive) that heighten tone beyond clinical description.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or insults by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about internal motives or intentions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Gives more emphasis to expert criticism but includes DHS defense; roughly proportional to article.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes main perspectives (experts, DHS, video/witnesses); no major viewpoint omitted.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically loaded terms (militarized, aggressive) and phrases that imply judgment.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Slightly amplifies negative framing with added adjectives, though overall follows article’s balance.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats experts and DHS similarly; no systematic deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Generally neutral but contains notable instances of inherited/added loaded framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures key sides but could more clearly separate different law-enforcement perspectives (e.g., former Border Patrol official) and note experts’ caveats about video limits.
